title: Abarema bauchei
taxonomy: Abarema bauchei is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae, commonly known as the legume or pea family. It belongs to the subfamily Faboideae and the genus Abarema. The genus Abarema is a group of trees and shrubs native to Central and South America. The species Abarema bauchei is endemic to Belize and Mexico. Further classification includes its tribe and order, which are Ingeae and Fabales, respectively.

intro: Abarema bauchei, also known as the Bauchi tree, is a species of flowering tree in the legume family. The plant is rich in various bioactive constituents, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The health benefits of Abarema bauchei include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and wounds. The most common herbal preparations made with Abarema bauchei include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used in traditional medicine in various parts of the world, particularly in Africa and Latin America.
